assumptions: All components run in a single Go process using goroutines and channels.
The task queue is an in-memory channel-based queue.
The data store is a simple file-based persistence (e.g., JSON file).
No external dependencies beyond the Go standard library.
constraints: Must be implementable in a single Go module.
No cloud services or external databases.
success_criteria: Worker processes tasks from the queue.
Worker persists results to the data store.
System runs without deadlocks or race conditions.

test_cases.name: TestEnqueueTask
test_cases.description: Verify that a task can be enqueued via the API gateway.

test_cases.name: TestWorkerProcessesTask
test_cases.description: Verify that the worker picks up and processes a task from the queue.

test_cases.name: TestDataPersistence
test_cases.description: Verify that processed task results are written to the data store.

test_cases.name: TestConcurrency
test_cases.description: Verify that multiple tasks can be processed concurrently without issues.
